projects.title: UAE Sugar Tax Market Analysis
projects.summary: Built a large-scale retail pricing dataset from 120+ websites to study the consumer impact of the UAE's sugar tax.
projects.longDescription:
THE PROBLEM

The UAE introduced a sugar tax, but its actual impact on consumer prices and product availability across different retail channels was not well-documented. Understanding the price pass-through rate required a dataset that did not yet exist.

WHAT I DID

Working as a Research Assistant at NYU Abu Dhabi, I designed and built a large-scale web scraping pipeline targeting 120+ JavaScript-rendered retail and e-commerce websites across the UAE.

  • Built a general-purpose scraping framework using Python and Playwright to handle dynamically rendered pages, anti-bot protections, and varied site architectures
  • Collected and normalized 1M+ price and product data points across beverages, food items, and related categories
  • Cleaned and structured the data for econometric analysis
  • Ran regression discontinuity (RD) and difference-in-differences (DiD) analyses in R and Stata to estimate price pass-through rates and demand elasticities

OUTPUT & RESULT

Produced one of the most comprehensive retail pricing datasets for tax impact analysis in the UAE market. The scraping framework is reusable across domains and continues to collect data for ongoing research.

WHY IT MATTERS

This project demonstrates the ability to build data infrastructure from scratch and turn it into actionable economic insight — a core skill for research, consulting, and data-driven policy work.

projects.title: Mukhosh Publications
projects.summary: Founded Bangladesh's first youth-oriented e-book publishing platform, growing it to 10K+ readers and a 4K community.
projects.longDescription:
THE PROBLEM

Young writers in Bangladesh had very few accessible platforms to publish their work. Traditional publishing was expensive and gatekept; digital publishing infrastructure for youth voices was essentially nonexistent.

WHAT I DID

I founded Mukhosh Publications in January 2021 as a digital-first publishing platform specifically for young Bangladeshi writers and creators.

  • Built and managed the full editorial pipeline: submission review, editing, layout, and digital distribution
  • Published 4 digital magazines and 2 full-length books
  • Recruited and mentored young writers, building a creator community of 4,000+ members
  • Developed distribution strategy across social media and digital channels, reaching 10,000+ readers
  • Managed all operations as a solo founder for the first year before bringing on team members

OUTPUT & RESULT

Over two years, Mukhosh became a recognized platform in the Bangladeshi youth literary space. 6 total publications, 10,000+ readers across Bangladesh and the diaspora, and a sustainable community of emerging writers.

WHY IT MATTERS

This project demonstrates the ability to build institutions, not just complete tasks. From concept to community to scale — I owned every part of a real product lifecycle.

projects.title: ADR Week Editorial Series
projects.summary: Created a recurring editorial series for Abu Dhabi Review with 10+ published articles on lifestyle, policy, and community.
projects.longDescription:
THE PROBLEM

Abu Dhabi Review needed regular, high-quality editorial content covering local events, community stories, and lifestyle topics relevant to the Abu Dhabi audience. The publication needed a writer who could create a consistent series with editorial voice and reliability.

WHAT I DID

During my internship at Abu Dhabi Review, I created and maintained the "ADR Week" editorial series — a recurring column I conceived, pitched, and executed.

  • Developed the series concept and editorial framework from scratch
  • Researched, wrote, and edited 10+ articles spanning lifestyle, local policy, and community coverage
  • Worked within a professional editorial review process with editors and fact-checking
  • Adapted writing style across different topics while maintaining consistent voice
  • Met weekly deadlines across the 4-month series run

OUTPUT & RESULT

10+ published articles on the Abu Dhabi Review platform. The series ran for 4 months and covered a range of topics that connected the Abu Dhabi community to relevant stories.

WHY IT MATTERS

This project demonstrates professional writing ability, editorial discipline, and the capacity to package information for public audiences consistently over time.

projects.title: India Sanitation & Wealth Analysis
projects.summary: Logistic regression analysis examining how household wealth shapes sanitation access in India. 636K+ households analyzed.
projects.longDescription:
THE PROBLEM

Access to improved sanitation in India varies dramatically by wealth and geography, but the interaction between household resources and infrastructure availability — particularly in rural areas — is not well understood at scale.

WHAT I DID

Using data from the National Family Health Survey (NFHS), I built a sequence of logistic regression models to examine how wealth, residence type, and household characteristics relate to improved sanitation access.

  • Cleaned and recoded NFHS survey variables following WHO specifications for 636,000+ households
  • Constructed binary indicators for improved sanitation and improved water access
  • Built 7 logistic regression models with increasing complexity to isolate wealth effects
  • Analyzed Wealth × Rural interaction terms to reveal how infrastructure constraints moderate the wealth-sanitation relationship
  • Produced descriptive statistics, model comparison tables, and coefficient interpretation

KEY FINDINGS

Wealth is strongly associated with higher odds of improved sanitation across all model specifications. However, rural households remain significantly less likely to have improved sanitation even after controlling for wealth. The interaction model reveals that infrastructure constraints in rural areas limit how effectively household resources translate into improved sanitation — meaning wealth buys less in rural India.

WHY IT MATTERS

This project demonstrates the ability to work with real large-scale survey data, apply appropriate econometric methods, and produce interpretable findings relevant to development economics and public policy.
